geometry.summary: A decimated marching-cubes shell derived from the TemplateFlow MNI152NLin2009cAsym brain mask.

activity.summary: The cortical shell has no activity model.

supports: Population-template tissue context for the co-registered visual-system layers.
assumptions: The shell is used as translucent context rather than subject anatomy or a cortical parcellation.
uncertainties: The exact source mask filename and offline meshing and decimation parameters are not checked in.

limitations.summary: This population-template surface must not be interpreted as an individual brain or as cytoarchitectonic boundaries.

geometry.summary: Bilateral shells extracted and simplified from the Jülich-Brain v3.0.3 maximum-probability map.

activity.summary: Region shells have no activity model.

supports: Approximate population-atlas locations and extents of the selected cytoarchitectonic regions.
assumptions: Winner-take-all maximum-probability labels are displayed as outline-like shells.
uncertainties: The source probability distribution is not visualized by the shell surface.

limitations.summary: Boundaries are population-atlas maximum-probability boundaries, not individual functional borders.

geometry.summary: Hand-authored eye-to-chiasm-to-LGN teaching curves and illustrative landmarks.

activity.summary: Evenly spaced display dots travel along the schematic crossing paths.

supports: The represented nasal-retinal paths cross at the optic chiasm before reaching the contralateral LGN.
assumptions: Control points, landmark positions, speed, spacing, color, and tube width are teaching choices.
uncertainties: Schematic coordinates are not measured anatomy.

limitations.summary: Uncrossed temporal-retinal pathways are omitted, so this is not a complete depiction of both hemifield projections.

geometry.summary: Left LGN-to-V1 streamlines tracked on the HCP-1065 nonlinear ICBM152-2009a FIB in decoded RAS+ millimetres; the displayed right side is mirrored at runtime.

activity.summary: Directed LGN-to-V1 events use a stochastic refractory display model on the streamline contours.

supports: The optic radiation is represented as a directed LGN-to-V1 projection.
assumptions: Runtime endpoint orientation uses the current data's validated higher-MNI-y LGN endpoint heuristic.
uncertainties: The release-companion T1 indicates the FIB's asymmetric variant, but no retained FIB build record binds it directly.
Post-processing is reproducible from the recovered intermediate; the DSI tracking run and complete tool manifest have not been replayed.

limitations.summary: Right-side anatomy is a sagittal mirror of left streamlines rather than independently tracked data.
Event rates, rhythms, bursts, time dilation, and travel speed are illustrative rather than measured LGN or V1 physiology.

licenses.label: WU-Minn HCP Open Access Data Use Terms

geometry.summary: Selected and resampled bilateral ICBM-2009a RAS+ streamlines from the HCP-1065 population tractography atlas, with unordered endpoints categorically assigned against the Jülich-Brain v3.0.3 maximum-probability map for optional filtering.

activity.summary: Seeded refractory code-like events choose one of two endpoint directions from explicit per-tract probability metadata.

supports: Sparse code-like population communication can be compared along the displayed named tract contours.
Endpoint filters can retain contours that touch or span selected displayed atlas-label sets without treating stored endpoint order as polarity.
assumptions: Version 1 samples a 50/50 direction per accepted event because no defensible quantitative human polarity ratio was found.
uncertainties: Diffusion MRI does not measure afferent versus efferent polarity or precise synaptic endpoints.
The categorical endpoint comparison overlays 2009a fibre coordinates with 2009c labels in common RAS millimetres without a template warp or a claim of voxel-grid equivalence.

limitations.summary: The 50/50 direction, channel rates, refractory recovery, and travel speed are disclosed display assumptions rather than measured axon proportions or physiology.

The classifier chooses the nearest nonzero MPM label. If its centre is within 2 mm and the label has a project region entity, the endpoint is known; a second distinct label within a 0.5 mm distance margin makes it ambiguous. Unsupported winning labels, background, and distant endpoints are unknown.

Qualified inspector links report only low-confidence nearest-surface endpoint proximity for the deterministic display sample; they are not terminations, pairwise connections, strengths, population probabilities, functions, or directions.

licenses.label: WU-Minn HCP Open Access Data Use Terms

geometry.summary: Real bilateral short contours tracked on the HCP-1065 nonlinear ICBM152-2009a FIB, filtered with an exact TemplateFlow-derived 2009c cortical ribbon, and assigned unordered endpoint classes from the Jülich-Brain v3.0.3 maximum-probability map in decoded RAS+ millimetres.

activity.summary: Independently phased bounded vibration moves around fixed contour homes with no assigned travel direction.

supports: The static grain conveys short-fibre orientation; vibration amplitude varies with supplied local fibre-length structure.
Endpoint filters can retain contours that touch or span selected displayed atlas-label sets while preserving direction-neutral geometry and vibration.
assumptions: Frequency, phase, scale factor, margins, and amplitude bounds are display choices.
uncertainties: The release-companion T1 indicates the FIB's asymmetric variant, but no retained FIB build record binds it directly.
Post-processing is reproducible from the recovered intermediate; the DSI tracking run and complete tool manifest have not been replayed.
The categorical endpoint comparison overlays 2009a fibre coordinates with 2009c labels in common RAS millimetres without a template warp or a claim of voxel-grid equivalence.

limitations.summary: Vibration is not measured activity, firing rate, conduction, or literal bidirectional signaling by one axon.

The classifier chooses the nearest nonzero MPM label. If its centre is within 2 mm and the label has a project region entity, the endpoint is known; a second distinct label within a 0.5 mm distance margin makes it ambiguous. Unsupported winning labels, background, and distant endpoints are unknown. These geometric classes do not establish named U-fibres or region-to-region relationships.

licenses.label: WU-Minn HCP Open Access Data Use Terms

geometry.summary: Text labels and viewer-authored stream groupings annotate other represented entities.

supports: Labels identify atlas regions, pathways, and viewer navigation groups.
assumptions: Grouping and color families are viewer-authored teaching metadata.
uncertainties: Some grouping labels may suggest functional or tract relationships beyond the source mesh names.

limitations.summary: Viewer-authored group names and arrows must not be interpreted as measured tract endpoints until separately reviewed.
